---
format: html
title: prepare_model_fold_input()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>prepare_model_fold_input</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Prepare Train and Test Model Inputs for One Fold</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Prepares one model fold without using held-out responses or predictors to
learn taxon filtering or predictor scaling. Spatial predictors must already
represent the training-only construction and held-out projection paths.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>prepare_model_fold_input(
  data_community_matrix = NULL,
  data_abiotic_wide = NULL,
  data_spatial_train = NULL,
  data_spatial_test = NULL,
  train_ids = NULL,
  test_ids = NULL,
  error_family = NULL,
  min_n_taxa = NULL,
  age_scale_mode = "z_score"
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_community_matrix">data_community_matrix</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric community matrix with named sample rows and taxon columns.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_abiotic_wide">data_abiotic_wide</code></td>
<td>
<p>Wide abiotic data frame with 'dataset_name', 'age', and predictor columns.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_spatial_train">data_spatial_train</code>, <code id="data_spatial_test">data_spatial_test</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional raw spatial predictor data frames for training and test samples.
Both must be supplied together and have sample identifiers as row names.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="train_ids">train_ids</code>, <code id="test_ids">test_ids</code></td>
<td>
<p>Disjoint character vectors of training and test sample identifiers.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="error_family">error_family</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar naming the model error family. Binomial responses are
converted to presence-absence before training-only taxon filtering.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="min_n_taxa">min_n_taxa</code></td>
<td>
<p>Positive integer giving the minimum retained training taxa.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="age_scale_mode">age_scale_mode</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar passed to 'scale_abiotic_for_fit()'. Supported values are
'&quot;z_score&quot;' and '&quot;center&quot;'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>Named list containing assembled training input, scaled test predictors,
aligned test observations, an explicit taxon mapping, and fold diagnostics.
</p>


<h3>Examples</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>data_community &lt;-
  base::matrix(
    base::c(0, 1, 0, 1),
    ncol = 1,
    dimnames = base::list(
      base::c("a__0", "b__0", "c__0", "d__0"),
      "taxon_a"
    )
  )
data_abiotic &lt;-
  base::data.frame(
    dataset_name = base::c("a", "b", "c", "d"),
    age = 0,
    temperature = base::c(8, 10, 12, 14)
  )
prepare_model_fold_input(
  data_community_matrix = data_community,
  data_abiotic_wide = data_abiotic,
  train_ids = base::c("a__0", "b__0", "c__0"),
  test_ids = "d__0",
  error_family = "binomial",
  min_n_taxa = 1L,
  age_scale_mode = "center"
)
</code></pre>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
